sections.label: Section 1.1074
sections.translation: Wherefore your cry of woe in Loxias ’ name ?

sections.label: Section 1.1075
sections.original: οὐ γὰρ τοιοῦτος ὥστε θρηνητοῦ τυχεῖν .
sections.translation: He is not the kind of god that has to do with mourners .

sections.label: Section 1.1076
sections.translation: Woe , woe , woe ! O Apollo , O Apollo !

sections.label: Section 1.1077
sections.translation: Once more with ill-omened words she cries to the god who should not be present at times of lamentation .

sections.label: Section 2.1080
sections.translation: Apollo , Apollo ! God of the Ways , [*] my destroyer ! For you have destroyed me — and utterly — this second time . [*]

sections.label: Section 2.1084
sections.original: μένει τὸ θεῖον δουλίᾳ περ ἐν [*] φρενί .
sections.translation: I think that she is about to prophesy about her own miseries . The divine gift still abides even in the soul of one enslaved .

sections.label: Section 2.1085
sections.translation: Apollo , Apollo ! God of the Ways , my destroyer ! Ah , what way is this that you have brought me ! To what a house !

sections.label: Section 2.1088
sections.original: πρὸς τὴν Ἀτρειδῶν : εἰ σὺ μὴ τόδ ἐννοεῖς ,
sections.translation: To that of Atreus ’ sons . If you do not perceive this , I ’ ll tell it to you . And you shall not say that it is untrue .

sections.label: Section 3.1090
sections.translation: No , no , rather to a god-hating house , a house that knows many a horrible butchery of kin , a slaughter-house of men and a floor swimming with blood .

sections.label: Section 3.1093
sections.original: ἔοικεν εὔρις ἡ ξένη κυνὸς δίκην
sections.translation: The stranger seems keen-scented as a hound ; she is on the trail where she will discover blood .

sections.label: Section 3.1095
sections.translation: Here is the evidence in which I put my trust ! Behold those babies bewailing their own butchery and their roasted flesh eaten by their father !

sections.label: Section 3.1098
sections.translation: Your fame to read the future had reached our ears ; but we have no need of prophets here .

sections.label: Section 4.1100
sections.translation: Alas , what can she be planning [*] ? What is this fresh woe she contrives here within , what monstrous , monstrous horror , beyond love ’ s enduring , beyond all remedy ? And help [*] stands far away !

sections.label: Section 4.1105
sections.translation: These prophesyings pass my comprehension ; but those I understood — the whole city rings with them .

sections.label: Section 4.1107
sections.translation: Ah , damned woman , will you do this thing ? Your husband , the partner of your bed , when you have cheered him with the bath , will you — how shall I tell the end ?

sections.label: Section 4.1110
sections.original: τάχος γὰρ τόδ ἔσται : προτείνει δὲ χεὶρ ἐκ
sections.translation: Soon it will be done . Now this hand , now that , she stretches forth !

sections.label: Section 4.1112
sections.original: οὔπω ξυνῆκα : νῦν γὰρ ἐξ αἰνιγμάτων
sections.translation: Not yet do I comprehend ; for now , after riddles , I am bewildered by dark oracles .

sections.label: Section 5.1114
sections.original: ἒ ἔ , παπαῖ παπαῖ , τί τόδε φαίνεται ;
sections.translation: Ah ! Ah ! What apparition is this ?

sections.label: Section 5.1115
sections.translation: Is it a net of death ? No , it is a snare that shares his bed , that shares the guilt of murder . Let the fatal pack , insatiable against the race , raise a shout of jubilance over a victim accursed ! [*]

sections.label: Section 5.1119
sections.translation: What Spirit of Vengeance is this that you bid

sections.label: Section 5.1120
sections.translation: raise its voice over this house ? Your words do not cheer me . Back to my heart surge the drops of my pallid blood , even as when they drip from a mortal wound , ebbing away as life ’ s beams sink low ; and death comes speedily .

sections.label: Section 5.1125
sections.original: ἂ ἄ , ἰδοὺ ἰδού : ἄπεχε τῆς βοὸς
sections.translation: Ah , ah , see there , see there ! Keep the bull from his mate ! She has caught him in the robe and gores him with the crafty device of her black horn ! He falls in a vessel of water ! It is of doom wrought by guile in a murderous bath that I am telling you .

sections.label: Section 5.1130
sections.original: οὐ κομπάσαιμ ἂν θεσφάτων γνώμων ἄκρος
sections.translation: I cannot boast that I am a keen judge of prophecies ; but these , I think , spell some evil . But from prophecies what word of good ever comes to mortals ? Through terms of evil their wordy arts

sections.label: Section 5.1135
sections.translation: bring men to know fear chanted in prophetic strains .

sections.label: Section 6.1136
sections.translation: Alas , alas , the sorrow of my ill-starred doom ! For it is my own affliction , crowning the cup , that I bewail . Ah , to what end did you bring me here , unhappy as I am ? For nothing except to die — and not alone . What else ?

sections.label: Section 6.1140
sections.translation: Frenzied in soul you are , by some god possessed , and you wail in wild strains your own fate , like that brown bird that never ceases making lament ( ah me !), and in the misery of her heart moans Itys , Itys ,

sections.label: Section 6.1145
sections.translation: throughout all her days abounding in sorrow , the nightingale .

sections.label: Section 6.1146
sections.translation: Ah , fate of the clear-voiced nightingale ! The gods clothed her in a winged form and gave to her a sweet life without tears [*] . But for me waits destruction by the two-edged sword .

sections.label: Section 6.1150
sections.translation: From where come these vain pangs of prophecy that assail you ? And why do you mold to melody these terrors with dismal cries blended with piercing strains ? How do you know the bounds of the path of your

sections.label: Section 6.1155
sections.translation: ill-boding prophecy ?

sections.label: Section 7.1156
sections.original: ἰὼ γάμοι γάμοι Πάριδος ὀλέθριοι φίλων .
sections.translation: Ah , the marriage , the marriage of Paris , that destroyed his friends ! Ah me , Scamander , my native stream ! Upon your banks in bygone days , unhappy maid , was I nurtured with fostering care ;

sections.label: Section 7.1160
sections.original: νῦν δ ἀμφὶ Κωκυτόν τε κἀχερουσίους [*]
sections.translation: but now by Cocytus and the banks of Acheron , I think , I soon must chant my prophecies .

sections.label: Section 7.1162
sections.original: τί τόδε τορὸν ἄγαν ἔπος ἐφημίσω ;
sections.translation: What words are these you utter , words all too plain ? A new-born child hearing them could understand . I am smitten with a deadly pain , while ,

sections.label: Section 7.1165
sections.translation: by reason of your cruel fortune , you cry aloud your pitiful moans that break my heart to hear .

sections.label: Section 7.1167
sections.original: ἰὼ πόνοι πόνοι πόλεος ὀλομένας [*] τὸ πᾶν .
sections.translation: O the sufferings , the sufferings of my city utterly destroyed ! Alas , the sacrifices my father offered , the many pasturing cattle slain to save its towers !

sections.label: Section 7.1170
sections.translation: Yet they provided no remedy to save the city from suffering even as it has ; and I , my soul on fire , must soon fall to the ground .

sections.label: Section 7.1173
sections.translation: Your present speech chimes with your former strain .

sections.label: Section 7.1175
sections.translation: Surely some malignant spirit , falling upon you with heavy swoop , moves you to chant your piteous woes fraught with death . But the end I am helpless to discover .

sections.label: Section 1178
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ὁ χρησμὸς οὐκέτ ἐκ καλυμμάτων
sections.translation: And now , no more shall my prophecy peer forth from behind a veil like a new-wedded bride ; but

sections.label: Section 1180
sections.original: λαμπρὸς δ ἔοικεν ἡλίου πρὸς ἀντολὰς
sections.translation: it will rush upon me clear as a fresh wind blowing against the sun ’ s uprising so as to dash against its rays , like a wave , a woe far mightier than mine . No more by riddles will I instruct you . And bear me witness , as , running close behind ,

sections.label: Section 1185
sections.translation: I scent the track of crimes done long ago . For from this roof never departs a choir chanting in unison , but singing no harmonious tune ; for it tells not of good . And so , gorged on human blood , so as to be the more emboldened , a revel-rout of kindred Furies haunts the house ,

sections.label: Section 1190
sections.translation: hard to be drive away . Lodged within its halls they chant their chant , the primal sin ; and , each in turn , they spurn with loathing a brother ’ s bed , for they bitterly spurn the one who defiled it . [*] Have I missed the mark , or , like a true archer , do I strike my quarry ?

sections.label: Section 1195
sections.translation: Or am I prophet of lies , a door-to-door babbler ? Bear witness upon your oath that I know the deeds of sin , ancient in story , of this house .

sections.label: Section 1198
sections.original: καὶ πῶς ἂν ὅρκος , πῆγμα [*] γενναίως παγέν ,
sections.translation: How could an oath , a pledge although given in honor , effect any cure ? Yet I marvel at you that ,

sections.label: Section 1200
sections.translation: though bred beyond the sea , you speak truth of a foreign city , even as if you had been present there .

sections.label: Section 1202
sections.original: μάντις μ Ἀπόλλων τῷδ ἐπέστησεν τέλει .
sections.translation: The seer Apollo appointed me to this office .

sections.label: Section 1203
sections.original: μῶν καὶ θεός περ ἱμέρῳ πεπληγμένος ; [*]
sections.translation: Before now I was ashamed to speak of this .

sections.label: Section 1204
sections.original: προτοῦ μὲν αἰδὼς ἦν ἐμοὶ λέγειν τάδε .
sections.translation: Can it be that he , a god , was smitten with desire ?

sections.label: Section 1205
sections.original: ἁβρύνεται γὰρ πᾶς τις εὖ πράσσων πλέον .
sections.translation: In prosperity all take on airs .

sections.label: Section 1206
sections.original: ἀλλ ἦν παλαιστὴς κάρτ ἐμοὶ πνέων χάριν .
sections.translation: Oh , but he struggled to win me , breathing ardent love for me .

sections.label: Section 1207
sections.original: ἦ καὶ τέκνων εἰς ἔργον ἠλθέτην [*] νόμῳ ;
sections.translation: Did you in due course come to the rite of marriage ?

sections.label: Section 1208
sections.translation: I consented to Loxias but broke my word .

sections.label: Section 1209
sections.translation: Were you already possessed by the art inspired of the god ?

sections.label: Section 1210
sections.translation: Already I prophesied to my countrymen all their disasters .

sections.label: Section 1211
sections.original: πῶς δῆτ ἄνατος [*] ἦσθα Λοξίου κότῳ ;
sections.translation: How came it then that you were unharmed by Loxias ’ wrath ?

sections.label: Section 1212
sections.original: ἔπειθον οὐδέν οὐδέν , [*] ὡς τάδ ἤμπλακον .
sections.translation: Ever since that fault I could persuade no one of anything .

sections.label: Section 1213
sections.original: ἡμῖν γε μὲν δὴ πιστὰ θεσπίζειν δοκεῖς .
sections.translation: And yet to us at least the prophecies you utter seem true enough .

sections.label: Section 1214
sections.translation: Ah , ah ! Oh , oh , the agony !

sections.label: Section 1215
sections.original: ὑπ αὖ με δεινὸς ὀρθομαντείας πόνος
sections.translation: Once more the dreadful throes of true prophecy whirl and distract me with their ill-boding onset . Do you see them there — sitting before the house — young creatures like phantoms of dreams ? Children , they seem , slaughtered by their own kindred ,

sections.label: Section 1220
sections.translation: their hands full of the meat of their own flesh ; they are clear to my sight , holding their vitals and their inward parts ( piteous burden !), which their father tasted . For this cause I tell you that a strengthless lion , wallowing in his bed , plots vengeance ,
